watercolors.label: Watercolors
watercolors.description: Original watercolor paintings by Imad Obegi — landscapes, botanicals, still life, and coastal scenes.

encaustics.label: Encaustics
encaustics.description: Paintings built up in layers of pigmented beeswax, fused with heat — an ancient medium that gives color an unusual depth and luminosity. Each piece is an original.

watercolors.title: Atlantic Crossing
watercolors.description: Muted blues and grays carry the weight of open water — the Atlantic in all its restless breadth, painted with loose, confident washes that capture the feeling of being far from shore.

watercolors.title: Autumn Arrival — Engine No. 3
watercolors.description: A vintage locomotive arrives through autumn color, warm ochres and rust bleeding into the surrounding foliage. History and movement, rendered in layered watercolor washes.

watercolors.title: Crimson Canna
watercolors.description: A close study of canna lilies, their petals unfolding in vivid reds and burnt oranges. The transparency of watercolor suits the luminous quality of the blooms — light seems to come from within.

watercolors.title: Emerald Isle — Beverly Waves
watercolors.description: Crashing surf along a coastline painted in layers of emerald, teal, and seafoam white. The painting holds the particular quality of Atlantic coastal light — bright and slightly cold, even in summer.

watercolors.title: Flame Lily Radiance
watercolors.description: The flame lily's reflexed petals — crimson fading to gold at the edges — are captured in confident watercolor strokes. A botanical study that doesn't sacrifice life for accuracy.

watercolors.title: Morning Light on Majolica
watercolors.description: Soft morning light falls across ornate majolica pottery, the rich glazes catching warmth and shadow. A quiet still life painted with the unhurried attention of early hours.

watercolors.title: Sentinel of the High Desert
watercolors.description: A lone saguaro stands against a wide desert sky, quiet and monumental. The painting captures the particular stillness of the high desert — the sense that something ancient is simply watching.

watercolors.title: The Crab Shack, Salter Path
watercolors.description: A weathered coastal shack in the fishing village of Salter Path, North Carolina, painted with the loose, direct strokes of plein air watercolor. Salt air and sun-bleached wood, caught before the light changed.

watercolors.title: The Spirit of Santa Monica
watercolors.description: The Santa Monica Pier at golden hour — the Ferris wheel, the Pacific, the particular glow of a California afternoon. Painted with warmth and a light hand, the way the place actually feels.

watercolors.title: The View from Home
watercolors.description: A familiar landscape seen from a place of belonging — the kind of view you stop noticing until you paint it. Rendered with quiet affection and the economy of someone who knows exactly what matters.

watercolors.title: The Vision Returns
watercolors.description: A richly layered composition where color and form do the work of language. The title suggests a return — something seen before, now seen again differently. One of the larger pieces in this collection.

watercolors.title: Wedgwood Jasperware Pitcher
watercolors.description: A careful still life study of a classic Wedgwood jasperware pitcher — its pale blue ground and white relief ornament rendered with attention to the subtle interplay of matte surface and ambient light.

encaustics.title: Alone in the Forest Sunrise
encaustics.description: First light filters through a dense forest canopy, its warmth captured in translucent encaustic layers that seem to glow from within. The trees emerge slowly from morning shadow, the way the eye adjusts.

encaustics.title: Aurora Imagined
encaustics.description: An imagined aurora fills the sky in radiant ribbons of green and violet, built up in encaustic layers that give the light an almost physical presence. What the eye cannot hold, the wax does.

encaustics.title: Bleeding Hearts
encaustics.description: The drooping heart-shaped blooms of bleeding heart, each one a perfect small pendant, rendered in translucent encaustic layers of rose and white. The name is melodramatic; the flower is not.

encaustics.title: Bouquet to Go
encaustics.description: A loosely arranged bouquet, bright and informal, caught in the moment before it finds a vase. Encaustic gives the petals a depth that other media rarely achieves — layers of color visible beneath layers of wax.

encaustics.title: Engraved Monarch
encaustics.description: A monarch butterfly rendered with an engraving-like precision, its wing patterns drawn into the encaustic surface before being fused. The result is both painting and drawing, both ancient technique and close observation.

encaustics.title: Fibroblast
encaustics.description: A microscopic world made visible — the cellular architecture of biological tissue abstracted into color and texture. Encaustic's layering quality mirrors the way cells build and repair.

encaustics.title: Fields of Mustard
encaustics.description: California's winter hills carpeted in wild mustard, their yellow brilliant against the dark green of the wet season. The encaustic surface gives the scene a warmth that the landscape itself also carries, unexpectedly, in January.

encaustics.title: Fiftieth Anniversary
encaustics.description: A painting marking fifty years — the encaustic surface, built layer by patient layer, is itself a metaphor for what accumulates over time. The image holds warmth, depth, and a weight appropriate to the occasion.

encaustics.title: Fire in The Sky
encaustics.description: The sky on fire at sunset, its oranges and magentas captured in fused wax that holds color with a particular luminosity. Encaustic was made for this — no other medium quite matches the way fire light fills space.

encaustics.title: Flower Field Under Mount Hood
encaustics.description: Oregon's wildflower meadows with Mount Hood rising white in the distance, the mountain's permanence set against the seasonal abundance below. Painted in encaustic layers that preserve the scene's depth and spaciousness.

encaustics.title: Fog Over Oakland
encaustics.description: Oakland's hills veiled in the marine layer that rolls in off the bay most mornings, the city's edges softened and its light gone silver. Encaustic's semi-translucent layers suit the subject perfectly.

encaustics.title: Good Luck Birds
encaustics.description: A composition of birds understood as symbols of fortune across many cultures — painted in encaustic with the clarity of folk art and the depth of the medium. Good luck made visible.

encaustics.title: Gothic Decorations
encaustics.description: Architectural ornament in the Gothic tradition — pointed arches, tracery, stone-carved flora — studied closely and translated into encaustic. The ancient building material and the ancient painting medium share an affinity.

encaustics.title: Green Abstract Fabric
encaustics.description: The rich folds and drape of green fabric, its movement frozen in encaustic layers that catch the light as actual cloth would. An exercise in pure paint: form, color, and texture without narrative.

encaustics.title: Heart Rising Over Poppy Field
encaustics.description: The artist's recurring heart motif lifts above a field of orange poppies, the encaustic sky luminous around it. Love and California wildflowers — both are brief, both are brilliant.

encaustics.title: Heart Rising Over Tuscany
encaustics.description: The heart motif over the rolling hills of Tuscany, its golden browns and sage greens recognizable to anyone who has seen that landscape. Encaustic gives the hills their dusty, afternoon-light warmth.

encaustics.title: Heart Rising Over the Ocean
encaustics.description: The heart ascends above open water, the ocean rendered in deep encaustic blues and the horizon kept clean. A small painting with simple ambitions — transcendence above the sea.

encaustics.title: Heart Rising over Mount Hood
encaustics.description: Oregon's dominant peak beneath the ascending heart, the mountain's permanence set in counterpoint to what the heart signifies. Encaustic layers give the snow cap its cold, flat light.

encaustics.title: Hugged by Clouds
encaustics.description: A form embraced by clouds, the encaustic medium's translucency giving the clouds a softness that feels genuinely atmospheric. To be surrounded by clouds is to be held by something vast and gentle.

encaustics.title: Lady Bugs Me
encaustics.description: A close study of a ladybug on branch, encaustic's layering allowing the shell's lacquered red to glow. The title admits what the painting knows — sometimes the small thing is the whole thing.

encaustics.title: Lavender Field
encaustics.description: A field of lavender stretches to the horizon, its purple rows built up in luminous encaustic layers that capture the flower's sun-warmed haze. The ancient medium and the ancient crop share something — both require time, patience, and heat.

encaustics.title: Leaves are Falling
encaustics.description: Autumn leaves mid-fall, their descent caught and held in encaustic. The turning colors — ochre, rust, amber — are what encaustic does best: color with depth, color with warmth built in.

encaustics.title: Lemon Lime Organelles
encaustics.description: A microscopic-inspired composition: the cellular structures of citrus, their essential geometry made visible and beautiful. The sour and the sweet of lemon and lime, translated into pigment and wax.

encaustics.title: Lemon Tree
encaustics.description: A lemon tree laden with fruit, the yellow of the lemons vivid against green leaves. Encaustic's saturated quality suits the painting's sense of abundance.

encaustics.title: Lone Snow Tree
encaustics.description: A single tree standing in snow, its bare branches making their own drawing against a white sky. Winter stillness — the kind that makes you stop.

encaustics.title: Milagros on Green
encaustics.description: The traditional Mexican milagros — small metal charms used as votive offerings — arranged on a ground of deep green encaustic. Faith made portable, devotion made beautiful.

encaustics.title: My Butterfly Collection
encaustics.description: A collector's arrangement of butterfly specimens, each one rendered in precise encaustic detail that captures the wings' patterns and iridescence. The reminder that beauty was once alive.

encaustics.title: Peacock on Display
encaustics.description: A peacock in full display, its iridescent eye-spotted train spread wide. Encaustic is ideal for this — its layers capture the way peacock feathers seem to contain their own light source.

encaustics.title: Poppy Field
encaustics.description: A field of poppies at their peak, the orange vivid against green stems. One of the larger works, its scale matching the ambition of the subject.

encaustics.title: Poppy Field after Monet
encaustics.description: Homage to Monet's poppy fields: the dappled light, the color pressing in from all sides. Encaustic gives the poppies Monet's luminosity through a different material logic.

encaustics.title: Red Poppies
encaustics.description: A close study of red poppies — their tissue-paper petals, their dark centers — painted in encaustic that gives the reds their full saturation. Not the field, but the flower itself.

encaustics.title: Red Poppy
encaustics.description: A single red poppy rendered with care, its petals cupped and its center visible. Encaustic's surface invites inspection — the closer you look, the more the layers reveal.

encaustics.title: Red Siding in Mexico
encaustics.description: The vivid red exterior walls of a Mexican building, their paint aged and weathered in ways that encaustic can render with surprising accuracy. Color that has lived through decades of sun.

encaustics.title: Reflected Trees
encaustics.description: Trees reflected in still water, the mirrored image as precise as the real one. Encaustic layers give the reflection a depth that makes the boundary between above and below unclear.

encaustics.title: San Francisco Panoramic
encaustics.description: The San Francisco Bay panoramic view: bridge, skyline, water, and light, rendered in encaustic across a long horizontal format. The city has been painted many times; encaustic finds in it something still new.

encaustics.title: Seed Pods in the Dirt
encaustics.description: The spent seed pods of various plants lying in bare earth, their architecture intricate and their season over. A quiet study of what remains after bloom, rendered with close attention.

encaustics.title: Serinic Marsh Land
encaustics.description: A marshland in stillness, the reeds and grasses built up in encaustic layers that give the scene its particular quiet. Tranquility rendered visible.

encaustics.title: Spiraling Out
encaustics.description: An abstract composition in spiral movement, the forms built up in encaustic layers that give the spiral its physical energy. Things expanding — or unwinding — both at once.

encaustics.title: Spirited Away
encaustics.description: A world encountered and a self transformed, held in encaustic color and form. The title evokes passage — arriving somewhere you didn't expect, leaving something behind.

encaustics.title: Star Papaya
encaustics.description: A cross-section of papaya, its seeds arranged in a perfect natural star, captured in tropical warmth. The fruit's interior — usually discarded — is here the entire subject.

encaustics.title: Stella Aurora
encaustics.description: Aurora and stars together: the celestial light show rendered in encaustic blues and greens that glow with an inner warmth. Named for the star-born dawn.

encaustics.title: Sunrise Over the Ocean
encaustics.description: The sun rising over open water, its light building from the horizon in encaustic layers that capture the dawn's particular gradient of color.

encaustics.title: Taken Away
encaustics.description: A painting about absence — what is here, and what is not. Encaustic's surface, with its translucent layers, is suited to subjects that are partly about what lies beneath.

encaustics.title: Tree of Persistence
encaustics.description: A tree that has survived — bent by wind, shaped by time, but upright still. Encaustic layers give the bark its texture and the whole composition its sense of endurance.

encaustics.title: Trees Sunrise
encaustics.description: Trees at dawn, their forms silhouetted or lit depending on their angle to the rising light. A small painting that holds a large moment.

encaustics.title: Windows on Tuscany
encaustics.description: The windows of a Tuscan farmhouse, their shutters and sills and the light they frame rendered in encaustic. The view through a window is always partly about the window itself.
